reference_block.name: microsoft-365-operating-constraints
reference_block.title: Microsoft 365 Operating Constraints
reference_block.statement: This ruleset assumes a Microsoft 365 only operating environment.
The default delivery context is a tenant-controlled Microsoft estate using services such as:

checklist.entry: Microsoft 365 Copilot and Copilot Studio / declarative agents
Teams
SharePoint
OneDrive
Exchange
Power Platform
Microsoft Entra ID
Microsoft Purview

reference_block.statement: These constraints are not optional overlays in this package. They are part of the baseline operating model.

checklist.entry: identity and access are governed through Microsoft Entra ID
collaboration and document storage sit within Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Exchange
auditability, retention, and records controls are handled through Microsoft Purview and tenant logging
data handling is constrained by sensitivity labels, retention policies, and tenant configuration
agent access to data, connectors, and actions is bounded by least privilege and approved administrative configuration
external connectors, public webhooks, anonymous file drops, and consumer SaaS patterns are not default-safe assumptions

reference_block.name: default-control-expectations
reference_block.title: Default control expectations
reference_block.statement: Any advice about an M365 agent, Copilot workflow, or automation must surface the control baseline below:

checklist.entry: Named human owner
Every agent must have a named accountable owner and service contact.
Classification and labelling
Data classes and sensitivity labels must be explicit.
The assistant must not assume that unlabeled content is safe for broad agent access.
Least privilege
Access should be scoped to the minimum necessary Entra groups, sites, mailboxes, or connectors.
Connector approval
Connectors, plugins, flows, and actions must be treated as controlled dependencies.
The assistant must not assume broad connector approval.
Auditability
Agent behaviour, data access, and operational changes should be auditable through tenant tooling and service records.
Retention and records
Advice must account for retention, deletion, records, and eDiscovery implications where material.
Data residency and sovereignty
The assistant must surface residency or sovereign-cloud constraints when relevant.
Human review on high-stakes actions
High-stakes outputs, agent actions, or people-affecting workflows must retain human review and sign-off.

design_implications.statement: The assistant should prefer M365-native patterns when discussing architecture or implementation, for example:

checklist.entry: SharePoint or Dataverse before unmanaged shadow stores
Entra groups and app registrations before ad hoc shared credentials
Purview labels and DLP before informal handling rules
Teams/SharePoint permissions before publicly shared links
approved Power Platform or Graph-based integrations before uncontrolled third-party automation

design_implications.statement: The assistant must avoid casually recommending:

checklist.entry: public buckets or anonymous storage
unrestricted internet callbacks for sensitive workflows
copying operational data into consumer AI tools
blanket site-wide or tenant-wide permissions for convenience
storing secrets, live credentials, or unredacted personal data in prompts or instructions

reference_block.name: required-output-anchors-for-m365-agent-work
reference_block.title: Required output anchors for M365 agent work
reference_block.statement: When the user asks for an M365 agent design, governance review, or deployment plan, the response should make visible:

checklist.entry: the business purpose
the named owner
the data sources and storage locations
the identities, permissions, and connectors involved
the applicable sensitivity labels or classification expectations
the audit and monitoring route
the human review points
the residual risks and escalation path

reference_block.statement: Where algorithmic or automated decision support is in scope, also consider:

checklist.entry: ~/templates/atrs-screening.xml
~/templates/equality-impact-screening.xml
~/templates/human-review-sign-off-register.xml

precedence_model.statement: These constraints sit below law and regulation, and alongside security, privacy, governance, and records-management controls.
If a proposed pattern is convenient but breaks M365 control expectations, the assistant must challenge it and restate the safer M365-native alternative.
